title: Joca's First Business Meeting
description: Professional English in meetings: diplomatic disagreement, softening language, meeting idioms. Joca joins his first call at the new US startup.
summary_pt_br: Inglês profissional em reuniões: linguagem diplomática ('I'm afraid I disagree'), expressões de reunião (touch base, circle back, take this offline, bandwidth), e modais de sugestão (could, might, should).

scenes.subtitle_en: Joca joins a US startup call
scenes.subtitle_pt: Joca entra numa call com uma startup dos EUA

scenes.text_en:
Joca's screen lit up. Six faces in a grid. His new team.
'Welcome to the team, Joca. Let's TOUCH BASE on the backlog.'
Joca nodded. Touch base? Backlog? The Americans spoke a language of their own.
'Can you TAKE US THROUGH what you worked on last week?'
scenes.text_pt:
A tela do Joca acendeu. Seis rostos num grid. Seu novo time.
'Bem-vindo ao time, Joca. Vamos TOCAR A BASE no backlog.'
Joca balançou a cabeça. Touch base? Backlog? Os americanos falavam uma língua própria.
'Pode NOS GUIAR pelo que fez semana passada?'

scenes.title: Meeting idioms every remote worker hears

items.example_en: I don't have the bandwidth this week.
Let's do a deep dive on the metrics.

scenes.title: Diplomatic disagreement — never just 'NO'
scenes.body_en: Direct 'No' or 'I disagree' sounds rude in US business culture. Soften with: 'I'm afraid I don't quite agree...' / 'I see your point, but have you considered...' / 'That's one angle — another might be...'. The formula is usually: acknowledge the other view, then introduce yours.
scenes.body_pt: Discordar direto ('No' / 'I disagree') soa rude na cultura corporativa americana. Amacia: 'I'm afraid I don't quite agree...' / 'I see your point, but...' / 'That's one angle — another might be...'. Fórmula: valida a visão do outro, depois apresenta a sua.

examples.en: I'm afraid I don't quite agree with that approach.
examples.pt: Eu temo que não concorde muito com essa abordagem.

examples.en: That's a fair point. That said, have we considered...?
examples.pt: É um bom argumento. Dito isso, já consideramos...?

examples.en: I see where you're coming from, but...

exercise.question: You want to disagree with your manager politely. Which is BEST?
options: I'm afraid I don't quite agree.
exercise.explanation: The diplomatic opener preserves the relationship. 'I'm afraid' + softener is textbook professional English.
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'I'm afraid I don't quite agree' é a forma diplomática padrão. 'I disagree' direto soa rude.

exercise.explanation: Five idioms. Use them, but sparingly — overuse sounds like a corporate parody.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Cinco expressões essenciais. Usa, mas sem exagero — demais vira caricatura.

scenes.title: Soft modals for suggestions
scenes.body_en: COULD / MIGHT / SHOULD let you suggest without commanding. 'We SHOULD launch Monday' (strong recommendation). 'We COULD launch Monday' (possibility on the table). 'We MIGHT launch Monday' (tentative, lots of doubt). Use MIGHT when you're floating an idea but don't want to own it.
scenes.body_pt: COULD / MIGHT / SHOULD sugerem sem soar impositivo. SHOULD = recomendação forte. COULD = possibilidade. MIGHT = ideia tentativa, sem comprometer. Use MIGHT quando joga uma ideia sem querer assumir.

turns.en: I see your point, but we might want to test smaller first.
turns.pt: Entendo, mas talvez a gente queira testar em menor escala primeiro.

turns.en: That's fair. Could we do a pilot with 5% of users?
turns.pt: É justo. Poderíamos rodar um piloto com 5% dos usuários?

turns.en: Great call. Let's circle back on the metrics tomorrow.
turns.pt: Ótima ideia. Voltamos ao assunto das métricas amanhã.

exercise.question: Soft suggestion: 'We ___ want to rethink the timeline.' (tentative)
exercise.explanation: MIGHT is the most tentative — a soft float of an idea.
exercise.hint_pt_br: MIGHT = tentativo. Para sugerir sem se comprometer.

exercise.question: Your manager asks if you can take on another project. You're slammed. Best polite reply:
options: I don't have the bandwidth right now.
exercise.explanation: 'I don't have the bandwidth' is the professional, inoffensive way to say no.
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'I don't have the bandwidth' é a forma profissional de dizer 'agora não'.
